🔬 Research: Quantum Communication & Information · Prof. Krishna Mohan Parattu · IIT Mandi
Who I Am
About Me

At the IIT Mandi viewpoint — green valleys of Uhl river below

I'm Himanshu Sharma, an M.Sc. Physics student at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Mandi, Himachal Pradesh. I was born and raised in Karauli, Rajasthan, and made my way to the mountains through a deep passion for physics and science.

I cleared IIT JAM 2025 with an All India Rank of 447, and was awarded the prestigious DST INSPIRE-SHE scholarship by the Government of India for being in the top 1% of RBSE board examinations.

Outside the classroom, I'm drawn to computational physics, optical experiments, and the kind of big questions that take you up mountain trails to think about.

Current Research

Working under the guidance of Prof. Krishna Mohan Parattu at IIT Mandi on Quantum Communication & Information — exploring the theoretical foundations of quantum entanglement, quantum channels, and information-theoretic aspects of quantum systems.

Moments
Through the Lens
Thumbs up from a Himachal hilltop — surrounded by the characteristic white-roofed buildings of a mountain town.
The sports ground — mountains framing every game, overcast skies adding drama to an ordinary afternoon.
A red laser optics setup — diffraction and lens experiments coming alive in the physics lab.
IIT Mandi campus on a monsoon day — yellow picnic tables, green cricket oval, and mist rolling in from the valley.
The tennis and basketball courts after rain, with cloud-draped peaks rising just beyond the wire fence.
A wide view of IIT Mandi's Kamand campus — academic blocks nestled in a green bowl, clouds hugging the ridge.
Recognition
Honours & Achievements

DST INSPIRE-SHE Scholar

Awarded by the Department of Science & Technology, Government of India, for securing a place in the top 1% of all students in the Class XII RBSE board examinations — a national recognition of academic excellence.

Govt. of India · DST

IIT JAM 2025 — AIR 447

Secured All India Rank 447 in the Joint Admission Test for M.Sc. (Physics) — a highly competitive national entrance examination. Gateway to the M.Sc. programme at IIT Mandi.

National · Physics
Documents
Certificates & Lab Work
Certificate from the Bookworms Club of IISER Berhampur — participated in the Mini Autumn-Winter School, completed all lectures, activities, and assignments. Signed by Dr. Tushar Mouli Chakraborti, Dept. of Earth & Environmental Sciences.
Hands-on optics experiment at the physics lab — a red laser, lenses, mirrors, and a diffraction slide on an optical bench. Part of the experimental physics curriculum at IIT Mandi.
